Title: Phenolic metabolism in Sarcandra glabra is mediated by distinct BAHD hydroxycinnamoyltransferases

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase superfamily; clade 6c; BBT-like; altered HxxxDA motif (HTISDA)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: 1kP transcriptome (sample code OSHQ); BLASTP-based candidate identification
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA amplification; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; protein purification; enzyme substrate screening; phylogenetic analysis; tissue expression analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tested tissues except no relative expression in young leaves; highest in old stems and young roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SgHCT-F was identified as a BAHD acyltransferase candidate and expressed in E. coli. 2. No donor or acceptor substrates could be identified despite extensive screening, so its metabolic role remains unresolved. 3. Phylogenetically it groups with benzoyl-CoA:benzyl alcohol O-benzoyltransferases, suggesting possible involvement in benzoyl ester formation rather than the main hydroxycinnamoyl ester pathways.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains a 91 bp intron and shows highest similarity (>75%) to predicted benzoyl-CoA:benzyl alcohol O-benzoyltransferases; benzyl benzoate formation was not observed experimentally.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in flowers, old leaves, young stems, old stems, young roots, and old roots; absent/undetectable in young leaves
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient light growth conditions; tissue-dependent expression

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase; HxxxDG and DFGWG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups, mainly from p-coumaroyl-CoA or caffeoyl-CoA, to the 5-OH of shikimic acid; also acylates quinic acid and hydroxyanthranilic acids at lower efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; highest catalytic efficiency among the characterized enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: young and old stems (highest caffeoyl-5-O-shikimic acid)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of p-coumaroyl-/caffeoylshikimic acids, mainly p-coumaroyl-5-O-shikimic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in young stems and young roots; moderate in old stems and old roots; not detected in flowers or leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in young stems and young roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 1kP transcriptome (sample code OSHQ); metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient light growth conditions; tissue context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; purified protein enzyme assays; LC-MS/HPLC product identification; kinetic characterization; tissue expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SgHST was biochemically identified as a shikimate-preferring BAHD acyltransferase. 2. It mainly forms p-coumaroyl-5-O-shikimic acid and can also produce caffeoyl-5-O-shikimic acid, with minor activity toward quinic acid and hydroxyanthranilic acids. 3. It showed the highest catalytic efficiency of the characterized S. glabra BAHD enzymes, supporting a major role in hydroxycinnamoylshikimate production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Hydroxyanthranilic acid amide formation in vitro suggests substrate promiscuity that could cause off-pathway products in engineering contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although 3-hydroxyanthranilic acid showed high affinity in vitro, corresponding metabolites were not detected in S. glabra. Authors infer p-coumaroyl-5-O-shikimic acid is the primary in vivo product.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase; HxxxDG and DFGWG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxycinnamoylquinate branch leading to chlorogenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream to downstream; direct product-forming step for chlorogenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups mainly to the 5-OH of quinic acid, producing p-coumaroyl-5-O-quinic acid and chlorogenic acid; can also acylate shikimic acid to multiple positional isomers at low efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of p-coumaroyl-/caffeoyl-5-O-quinic acids, including direct formation of chlorogenic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tissues; highest in old stems; also high in leaves and flowers; lowest in old roots and young stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broad expression across tissues, highest in old stems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 1kP transcriptome (sample code OSHQ); metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient light growth conditions; tissue context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; purified protein enzyme assays; LC-MS/HPLC product identification; kinetic characterization; tissue expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SgHQT1 was identified as the main chlorogenic-acid-forming HQT in S. glabra. 2. It predominantly synthesizes p-coumaroyl-5-O-quinic acid and caffeoyl-5-O-quinic acid, while also producing multiple shikimate esters under extended conditions. 3. Its broad tissue expression supports contribution to chlorogenic acid accumulation in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate promiscuity can generate multiple shikimate isomers and dicaffeoylshikimate derivative under prolonged incubation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Caffeoyl-CoA showed somewhat higher affinity than p-coumaroyl-CoA with quinic acid constant, but p-coumaroyl-CoA gave higher catalytic efficiency. Product diversification in vitro includes three positional shikimate isomers.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase; HxxxDG motif; DFGYG variant
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream; direct product-forming step for cryptochlorogenic acid branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups mainly to the 4-OH of quinic acid, yielding p-coumaroyl-4-O-quinic acid and cryptochlorogenic acid; also forms 4-O-shikimate esters.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; low activity and very low expression likely limit metabolite levels
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: flowers (reported low levels up to 0.11% dry weight)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes specific formation of p-coumaroyl-/caffeoyl-4-O-quinic acids, including cryptochlorogenic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Overall barely expressed; highest in old stems after adjusted PCR conditions; very low in roots, stems, and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very low overall, highest in old stems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 1kP transcriptome (sample code OSHQ); metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient light growth conditions; acyl migration control experiments at pH 6.0, 7.0, and 8.0 support genuine enzymatic 4-O product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; purified protein enzyme assays; LC-MS/HPLC product identification; kinetic characterization; acyl-migration control experiments; tissue expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SgHQT2 was shown to specifically acylate the 4-OH of quinic acid, generating p-coumaroyl-4-O-quinic acid and cryptochlorogenic acid. 2. The study provides the first thorough characterization of an enzyme forming cryptochlorogenic acid. 3. Its low enzymatic activity and weak transcript abundance are consistent with low cryptochlorogenic acid accumulation in S. glabra.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower catalytic efficiency than the other characterized BAHD enzymes may constrain engineering performance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The authors ruled out that the 4-O products were mainly artifacts of nonenzymatic acyl migration by dedicated pH/time incubation controls.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase; HxxxDG and DFGWG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Rosmarinic acid and hydroxyphenyllactate ester branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream to downstream; forms direct precursor and target-related products
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: p-coumaroyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid; caffeoyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid; p-coumaroyl-3',4'-dihydroxyphenyllactic acid; rosmarinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups mainly from p-coumaroyl-CoA or caffeoyl-CoA to hydroxyphenyllactic acid acceptors, producing rosmarinic acid and precursor esters.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product and immediate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: all tested tissues; highest rosmarinic acid in old roots; caffeoyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid highest in young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes synthesis of rosmarinic acid and related hydroxyphenyllactic acid esters.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tested tissues; highest in young roots and flowers; lower in roots and other organs but broadly present
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broad expression across flowers, leaves, stems, and roots; strongest in young roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 1kP transcriptome (sample code OSHQ); metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient light growth conditions; tissue context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; purified protein enzyme assays; LC-MS/HPLC product identification; kinetic characterization; tissue expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SgRAS synthesizes rosmarinic acid and multiple hydroxyphenyllactic acid precursors from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA donors. 2. It prefers p-coumaroyl-CoA over caffeoyl-CoA and likely forms p-coumaroyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid as the major initial product in vivo. 3. Broad transcript abundance correlates with widespread rosmarinic acid and precursor accumulation in S. glabra tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Compared with some Lamiaceae RAS enzymes, catalytic efficiency is relatively low, which may limit flux in heterologous engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer that caffeoyl substitution pattern may be finalized by meta-hydroxylation of p-coumaroyl esters; sequence differs slightly from a recently reported S. glabra RAS allele.
